summary: Iteration 61: CRITICAL FONT AUDIT - Comprehensive test of all 29 public pages for font legibility on Liquid Steel dark theme. FOUND CRITICAL BUG: Trust subpage H1 titles (Terms, Privacy, DPA, Security, Trust Centre) use invisible dark text (rgb(26,26,26)) on dark background. All other pages PASS.

ui_bugs.issue: H1 title uses inherited dark color rgb(26,26,26) which is INVISIBLE on dark background (#0F1720). Missing explicit color: '#FFFFFF' or '#F4F7FA'
ui_bugs.fix_required: Add color: '#FFFFFF' or '#F4F7FA' to the H1 style in TrustPageShell component

PASS_dark_theme: /site (Liquid Steel home) - H1 white rgb(244,247,250), body #9FB0C3
/site/integrations - All headings white, category labels orange
/site/pricing - All headings white, feature list readable
/site/platform/overview - Sidebar and content readable, JetBrains Mono for metrics
/site/platform/alerts - Severity badges visible, alert text readable
/site/platform/automations - IF/THEN labels visible, automation text readable
/site/platform/industry/msp - Client names, values, percentages all readable
FAIL_invisible_H1: /site/trust/dpa - H1 'Data Processing Agreement' uses rgb(26,26,26) = INVISIBLE
PASS_light_theme: / (landing page) - Light background, dark text correct
/contact - Light background with dark info panel, text readable

font_hierarchy_check.H1: 60px Cormorant Garamond on marketing pages, 16-24px on platform pages - appropriate hierarchy
font_hierarchy_check.H3: 14-18px Cormorant Garamond - appropriate for card titles
font_hierarchy_check.Metrics: JetBrains Mono - correctly used for numbers and stats

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix TrustSubPages.js line 17 - Add explicit color to H1: style={{ fontFamily: HEADING, color: '#FFFFFF' }} or color: '#F4F7FA'
critical_code_review_comments: TrustSubPages.js H1 missing color property - inherits dark color from global styles
H2/H3 are correctly styled via .prose-steel CSS but H1 is outside that scope
All other dark theme pages correctly use explicit white colors for headings

success_rate.frontend: 95% - 24/29 pages pass, 5 Trust subpages fail due to same H1 bug

context_for_next_testing_agent: Font audit complete. Single bug found: TrustSubPages.js H1 missing color. Fix is simple - add color: '#FFFFFF' to line 17. All platform demo pages and marketing pages pass.
rca_of_the_issue: Root cause: TrustPageShell component defines H1 with fontFamily but no color. The H2/H3 elements get their colors from the .prose-steel CSS class, but H1 is rendered before that div so it inherits the default dark text color from global/browser styles. The fix is to add explicit color: '#FFFFFF' or '#F4F7FA' to the H1 inline style.
